text:
9 APRIL 1958

I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC

East German, Polish, and Czech foreign ministers meet
token_text: 9 april 1958 i the communist bloc east german polish and czech foreign ministers meet

text:
Prague, presumably to plan campaign against West German nuclear armament.

Soviet submarines may be active in Atlantic as far west as Newfoundland and in Pacific off US west coast.

Peiping's purge of rightist officials seems to be over for time being.

II. ASIA-AFRICA

Israelis apparently resume drainage operations in Hula area with minor adjustments in conforming with UN survey.

Iran exploring with Pakistan and Turkey advisability of Middle East non-Arab federation to counter Egyptian influence; Afghanistan might also be invited.

IBRD President Black believes Nasir has "real desire" to settle with Suez Canal shareholders soon.

III. THE WEST

Cuba - Batista and Trujillo renew charge that Castro is instrument of international Communism. Charge is largely unsubstantiated.

West Germany appears to have modified repatriation demands in entering trade, repatriation, and consular agreement with USSR.

Italy - Change in Italian Communist leadership unlikely to affect Communist chances in 25 May elections

text:
I. THE COMMUNIST BLOC

Soviet Submarine Activity

An undetermined number of units of the Soviet submarine force are apparently conducting patrols or exercises outside their normal areas of operation in both the Atlantic and Pacific.

The Soviet survey ship Ekvator, subordinate to the Northern Fleet, has been cruising in the North Atlantic since 20 March and is now located south of Iceland. This ship engaged in similar activity during July and August 1957 when it appeared to have some connection with extensive Soviet submarine operations in the Atlantic at that time. Moreover, three contacts, tentatively evaluated as possible submarines, have been reported off the Shetland Islands, Iceland, and Newfoundland.

at least two Pacific Fleet submarines have been on long-range patrol since at least 25 March, possibly as far as the US west coast. One of these submarines is based at Petropavlovsk and is probably a Z-class. The other is apparently based at Vladivostok
